region_of_origin: Tropical regions; widely produced and consumed in Southeast Asia including Singapore

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of mango juice drink is approximately 250 ml (one standard glass), providing about 113 kcal and 28 g of carbohydrates, predominantly from sugars.

nutrient_source.title: Mango juice drink — Singapore HPB Foodo nutrient database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D03070020 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken from this source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 51 for mango juice / mango juice drink is consistent with values reported in the University of Sydney GI Database for mango juice and fruit juice drinks with similar sugar content. The input record cited GI 51 (low) with GL 5.7 at an unspecified serving size. GL has been recalculated here for a 250 g serving: GL = (51 × 11.2 × 2.5) / 100 = 14.3, rounded to 14.2. The original input GL of 5.7 appears to correspond to a smaller reference portion (~100 g); the 250 g serving GL is used here for clinical relevance.

hypertension.key_factor: High free sugar content (10.12 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage; sodium is low at 15 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Despite low sodium (15 mg/100 g), the high free sugar load (10.12 g/100 g) in this sweetened juice drink independently elevates blood pressure risk through insulin-mediated sodium retention and sympathetic nervous system activation; individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid sugar-sweetened beverages and choose water or unsweetened drinks instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 10.12 g free sugar per 100 g and negligible dietary fibre (0.3 g/100 g); liquid sugar from concentrate is rapidly absorbed
type2_diabetes.guidance: This mango juice drink is a sugar-sweetened beverage delivering 10.12 g of rapidly absorbed free sugars per 100 g with only 0.3 g fibre, causing rapid postprandial glycaemic excursions; individuals with or at risk of type 2 diabetes should avoid juice drinks and choose whole mango in small portions to benefit from the fibre matrix that slows glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar content (10.12 g/100 g) in a liquid form with negligible fibre; regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides and CVD risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened juice drinks like this product is associated with elevated serum triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and increased cardiovascular disease risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should replace sweetened juice drinks with water, unsweetened beverages, or whole fruit to reduce added sugar intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High free sugar (10.12 g/100 g) with negligible fibre promotes pro-inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high free sugar content of this juice drink promotes systemic inflammation relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; individuals with RA should avoid sweetened beverages and choose whole fruit with intact fibre and antioxidant content instead.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mango juice drink is a ready-to-drink sweetened beverage made from mango juice concentrate diluted with water and combined with added sugars or sweeteners. It is widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia as a packaged or dispensed drink, known in Chinese as 芒果汁饮料, in Malay as Minuman jus mangga, in Tamil as மாம்பழ சாறு பானம், and in Hindi as आम का जूस ड्रिंक. The beverage has a sweet, tropical mango flavour with a thin, watery texture compared to whole mango or mango nectar. It is not equivalent to 100% mango juice; the juice concentrate base means the natural fibre matrix of whole mango is absent. Per 100 g, it provides 45 kcal, 11.2 g carbohydrate (of which 10.12 g is sugar), 0.07 g protein, 0.02 g fat, 0.3 g dietary fibre, and 15 mg sodium. The high free sugar content (10.12 g/100 g) classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB). Clinically, it is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes due to rapid glycaemic impact from liquid free sugars without a fibre buffer. It also poses moderate concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease through sugar-driven mechanisms independent of its low sodium content. The glycemic index is approximately 51 (low category), but the glycemic load per 250 ml serving is approximately 14, which is clinically meaningful. It is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Common serving context includes snacks and casual refreshment occasions.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in mango juice drink?
Is mango juice drink good for high blood pressure?
Is mango juice drink the same as mango juice?
Mango juice drink vs whole mango nutrition

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id D03070020, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95). GI value of 51 is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for mango juice drinks. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and plausible. Multilingual names added for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 coverage for a beverage widely consumed in Singapore.
notes: saturated_fat_g is reported as 0.0: this is a confirmed trace/negligible value for a dilute juice drink with only 0.02 g total fat per 100 g; confirmed zero per HPB source. iron_mg is reported as 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB source for this product. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-derived juice drink with no animal-derived ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific mango juice drink product are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for D03070020. GI of 51 is an analogous estimate consistent with University of Sydney GI Database values for mango juice and similar fruit juice drinks; the input record cited GI 51 with GL 5.7 (likely per 100 g reference portion); GL recalculated to 14.2 for a 250 g serving. Multilingual local names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been added to satisfy G6 and Q1/Q8 requirements, as this beverage is widely consumed in Singapore and the validator required these language fields to be populated.
